metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_729
metadata.comment: Rationale: The authors use a screen to identify proteins in Drosophila melanogaster that carry random chemically induced (EMS) mutations that affect synaptic transmission in photoreceptors. This led to the identification of tweek. To demonstrate that tweek is involved in endocytosis, the authors used neuromuscular junctions from third instar larvae. In figure 3C/D they demonstrate that tweek mutants have decreased uptake of the dye FM1-43 into the presynaptic terminal in response to a 10Hz stimulation. Uptake of FM1-43 is well established as a measure of synaptic vesicle endocytosis. In figure 3E/F they use siRNA to knock down tweek, which also leads to decreased FM1-43 uptake.. Experimental description: From results, "To identify proteins that affect synaptic transmission and plasticity, we performed genetic screens using eyFLP technology ( Newsome et al., 2000; Stowers and Schwarz, 1999 ; Verstreken et al., 2003). These screens allow us to identify flies that carry random chemically induced (EMS) mutations that affect synaptic transmission in photoreceptors, circumventing the organismal lethality associated with these mutations. Flies with mutant eyes were screened by recording electroretinograms (ERGs)...".
